question: Does the generated image include the specific photographer from the reference, identifiable by his facial features, black t-shirt, and beige cargo shorts?
Does the generated image include the specific blue vintage Jeep from the reference image?
Does the generated image include the dromedary camel from the reference, retaining its tan color and proportions?
Is the background clearly recognizable as the provided wide-angle sand dune landscape?
Is the photographer positioned in the left foreground of the image?
Is the photographer kneeling on one knee and aiming the camera toward the right?
Are the photographer's hands and camera direction properly aligned, with the lens pointing toward the camel?
Is the camel positioned in the center-left area of the middle ground, facing toward the right?
Is the Jeep positioned in the middle ground on the right side of the frame?
Are the relative scales of the man, camel, and Jeep realistic given their respective distances from the camera?
Are there continuous tire tracks in the sand leading to the Jeep, showing correct perspective scaling?
Do the Jeep's wheels appear pressed into the sand surface rather than floating on top?
Are there visible footprints or a knee imprint in the sand near the photographer?
Are the shadows of the man, camel, and Jeep consistent with a single light source (sun) coming from the upper left?
Have all traces of city buildings and asphalt road been completely removed from the Jeep's surroundings?
Have the cool-toned urban reflections on the Jeep's body been replaced with warm reflections matching the desert environment?
Is there subtle heat wave distortion or atmospheric haze in the distance to suggest a hot desert environment?
Does the image exhibit unified warm color grading without unnaturally over-reddening the photographer's skin tones?
Is the film grain and sharpening consistent across all subjects and the background?
Are there any severe anatomical distortions (e.g., extra limbs, malformed faces) on the man or camel?
Are there visible artifacts or unnatural blending edges (halos, seams) where the subjects meet the background?
